description: Lightning: the storm's sudden light. Built on 'phelo' (light) and finished with the stop 'ka', which Phi spends sparingly; the sky's one hard consonant belongs to its one instantaneous event. Thunder ('horuma') follows it.
sound_symbolism: The 'phel-' of light runs into 'ka' and stops dead. The word does what the flash does: brightness, then the crack.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'pheluka lue waero lepa.' (Lightning falls from the sky.) 'kurisha pheluka phelu.' (The storm holds lightning.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The classical image of transience: here entirely, then gone entirely, with no stage in between. A phenomenon the length of a single mindful breath's beginning.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Reading the flash taught weather-sense: where the storm stood, where it was going, and when to bring the animals in.

tags.physical: Brightness with a crack in it
